---
title: "Part 4: Boundary Behavior and Convex Hull"
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

[Back to regularizeNd series home](index.qmd)

[Previous: Part 3](part-03-interpolation-and-solver-behavior.qmd)

This part explains why regularized lookup tables can remain useful near and beyond sparse-data boundaries.

## Practical Goal

- Examine behavior near the convex hull of scattered data.
- Compare to common scattered interpolation behavior near edges.
- Interpret why smoothness equations shape extrapolation trends.

## Example Anchor

Primary script: `regularizeNd/Examples/example_scattered_data_near_convex_hull.m`

## Suggested Walkthrough

1. Visualize sample coverage and hull boundary.
2. Query near and beyond sparse regions.
3. Compare trend consistency and smoothness.

## Attribution

This educational progression is inspired by Peter Goldstein's regularization articles and related prior tools.

## Next

[Part 5: Scaling to 4D and Beyond](part-05-scaling-to-4d-and-beyond.qmd)
